top_issues.title: Multi Mode automated manual gearbox control faults
top_issues.description: The Multi Mode automated manual transmission can suffer from erratic behaviour in automatic mode, including unexpected high-revving, failure to change gear smoothly at low speeds, and getting stuck in first gear. Owners report the gearbox sometimes requires switching to manual mode and back to automatic to reset normal operation. This is a control system fault rather than mechanical wear, but it can be unpredictable and potentially unsafe.
typical_symptoms: Engine revving to 8,000 rpm without warning
Transmission refusing to change up from first gear at low speed
Shuddering halt when accelerating from a junction
Neutral light flashing and gearbox refusing to engage
Problem resolves temporarily after switching to manual mode

top_issues.title: DPF regeneration problems on 2.2-litre diesel engines
top_issues.description: The 2.2-litre diesel Auris can experience difficulties with diesel particulate filter regeneration, leading to overfuelling. This overfuelling can damage piston rings and cylinder bores, which is a serious engine fault. Toyota extended the warranty to five years on affected vehicles to cover this issue, suggesting it was a known problem.
typical_symptoms: DPF warning light on dashboard
Excessive fuel consumption
Rough idle or loss of power
Black smoke from exhaust
Engine management warning light

top_issues.title: Water ingress into spare wheel well via cabin vent flaps
top_issues.description: The one-way cabin vent flaps located behind the rear bumper sides can fail, allowing water to leak into the spare wheel well. This can lead to rust and corrosion of the spare wheel and surrounding metalwork. Toyota later issued modified flaps to address the problem, but earlier cars are at risk.
typical_symptoms: Water pooling in spare wheel well
Rust on spare wheel or wheel well
Musty smell from boot area
Visible water staining inside boot

top_issues.title: Hybrid battery discharge when vehicle left unused
top_issues.description: Auris HSD hybrid models can suffer from rapid battery discharge if left unused for two to three weeks. The 12-volt auxiliary battery gradually loses its capacity to hold a full charge if the car is not driven regularly. This can leave the vehicle unable to start and may require professional recovery or trickle charging.
typical_symptoms: Vehicle will not start after a period of non-use
Dim dashboard lights when attempting to start
Clicking sound when turning the key
Battery warning light on dashboard

top_issues.title: Steering column universal joint wear and failure
top_issues.description: The steering column universal joint can wear and develop a knocking or clunking sound, particularly when driving over rough surfaces or when the steering wheel is moved. This is not a safety-critical fault but requires replacement of the entire steering column, which is a costly repair.
typical_symptoms: Knocking or clunking noise from steering column
Noise worse over bumpy roads
Noise when steering wheel is jiggled
Noise present throughout the steering range

overall_reliability_note: The first-generation Auris is generally a reliable family hatchback, but early Multi Mode automated manual variants can suffer from control faults, diesel models require careful DPF management, and hybrid variants need regular use to maintain battery health.
